lines: "They are dead, and the wheat is too polite now. I knew before you said it."
"Thank you for not wrapping it in sugar. Sugar brings ants, and I cannot slap them."
"Safe, good. I knew the Choir would speak with holy sternness, preferably from a considerable distance."
"When rapture comes, I may only tax them a little. For rope damages."
"You saw the yard, so do not tidy your face before answering; a hundred harvests taught me that look."
"Are they well? Tell me straight or kind, because I no longer know which I deserve."

effects.log: Eleazar gives up the location of his old forest stash.

lines: A calcified man stands lashed above the wheat. His face moves when he hears your boots in the furrows.
"Eleazar Carbo. You saw the yard, so do not tidy your face before answering; a hundred harvests taught me that look."
"Are they well? Tell me straight or kind, because I no longer know which I deserve."

effects.log: Eleazar gives up the location of his old forest stash.
Eleazar gives up the location of his old forest stash.
Eleazar gives up the location of his old forest stash.

lines: "Have you checked the Carbo house? I would go, but the wheat has promoted me to permanent staff."
"If they are angry, say I kept watch. I still know the sound of their door."

effects.log: Eleazar rustles in his ropes, pleased and offended at once.

lines: A calcified man stands lashed above the wheat. His legs are stone, his ribs are pale shell, and his face still works in a narrow patch of living skin.
"Hail, traveler. Eleazar Carbo, field hand. The first Holts after the Stilling put me here to scare birds off Carbo grain."
"I promoted myself to advanced scarecrow after year ten. When I am king, my first law will make birds illegal."
"If Gideon sent you, tell him I am nearly royal."

choices.label: Ask why he is in the field

effects.log: Eleazar rustles in his ropes, pleased and offended at once.

lines: "My brother. If the road still has use for him, he is pointing at Hallowfen and taking insults from pilgrims."
"He worries about me. I am busy worrying about the house. The cups stopped clinking after the Choir came through."

choices.label: Ask why he is in the field

lines: "The first Holts after the Stilling lashed me here. Practical people. They had grain, crows, and one great-granduncle who could no longer run from work."
"That became the custom: keep the birds off Carbo wheat, take the cup on a pole, wear the winter blanket, shout when wings came down."
"I hated them for thirty years. Then their children started calling me Uncle Eleazar, and hate had to share the field."
"The Choir came singing through the wheat and called me holy. Properly holy, not village holy, which mostly meant no one wanted to touch me."
"They said rapture is coming and calcified men rise first, with Gideon pointing armies. I would rather walk home and ask why the cups stopped clinking at breakfast."
"Of course I believe it; a man must keep an ambition. Mine used to involve boots, but standing in the yard without ropes will do."
"I will be merciful. No shouting at the children. No ropes for anyone who can still feel them."
"Gideon doubts anything that arrives singing. He had more road than I did, and the road taught him mean."
"I had wheat and kitchen smoke on clear mornings. That made me easier to fool."
"Mockery noted. I will remember it after ascension, if remembering still belongs to me."
"Still, check the family. Even a future prince keeps accounts."
"The Carbo house by the yard is mine by blood and habit. They did not start this cruelty. They learned to live around it."
"The Choir said they would speak to them about disrespect. I hope that means stern words and not knives. Please check."

effects.log: Eleazar goes quiet long enough for the wheat to rasp around him.

lines: "Perhaps. I said worse in year twelve."
"Check anyway. Anger never granted anyone permission to vanish a household; I learned that after year thirteen."
"Dead. Ah."
"So much for the Choir's stern words; they left the yard quiet instead."
"I had a stash before the Stilling. Forest side of the branch road, under roots beside a split stump. Take it. I have no pockets, and no wish to enrich the crows."
"Safe, you say. I will hold that word carefully and try not to ask why your face gave it away so cheaply."
"When I rise, I will visit them first and be gracious. I have practiced that word for years."
"I had a stash before the Stilling. Forest side of the branch road, under roots beside a split stump. Take it for the news. I cannot exactly fetch it with dignity."
"You made the truth wear boots before it came in."
"Leave the cruelty standing if you need the company. I have endured worse."
"Forest side of the branch road, under roots beside a split stump. My old stash is there. Take it and go before I mistake your voice for theirs."
